name: November
description: Thanksgiving, Veterans Day, Guy Fawkes, the Beaver Moon, and JFK — the 11th month that's named for nine.

questions.q: How many days does November have?

source.notes: Per the rhyme: 'Thirty days hath September, April, June, and November.'

questions.q: From which Latin numerical prefix does November get its name?

source.original_a: Derived from the Latin word "novem," meaning nine
source.notes: November was the ninth month before January and February were added.

questions.q: What is the popular name for the November full moon?

source.notes: Also called the Frost Moon. Named for when beaver-trapping season began.

questions.q: On what day of the week is U.S. Election Day always held?
choices: Tuesday after the first Monday of November

source.notes: Set in 1845 to give farmers time to travel after Sunday worship and end of the harvest.

questions.q: On which day in November is Veterans Day observed in the U.S.?

source.notes: Marks the end of WWI hostilities (the 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month, 1918). Called Remembrance Day in much of the Commonwealth.

questions.q: On which day of November is U.S. Thanksgiving celebrated?

source.original_a: Election results or State of the Union Addresses (if during election years)
source.notes: Set by Congress to the fourth Thursday in 1941.

questions.q: Which U.S. president officially established Thanksgiving as a national holiday in 1863?

source.notes: Proclaimed a 'day of Thanksgiving and Praise' during the Civil War.

questions.q: On what date in November 1963 was U.S. President John F. Kennedy assassinated?

source.notes: In Dallas, Texas.

questions.q: Which date in November marks the historic 1989 fall of the Berlin Wall?

source.notes: Berlin Wall opened on the night of November 9, 1989.

questions.q: Which UK holiday on November 5 commemorates a foiled 1605 plot to blow up Parliament?

source.notes: 'Remember, remember, the fifth of November, gunpowder, treason and plot…'

questions.q: On which days does Mexico's 'Día de los Muertos' (Day of the Dead) traditionally fall?

source.notes: Aligned with Catholic All Saints' (Nov 1) and All Souls' (Nov 2) days.

questions.q: Which Christian feast falls on November 1 each year?

source.notes: Halloween (October 31) is 'All Hallows' Eve' — the night before.

questions.q: Which spectacular mid-November meteor shower can produce 'meteor storms' every 33 years?

source.notes: Most famously the 1833 storm with thousands of meteors per hour.

questions.q: Which annual fitness/charitable November tradition has men growing mustaches to raise awareness for men's health?

source.notes: Movember Foundation focuses on prostate cancer, testicular cancer, and men's mental health.

questions.q: Which November anniversary on November 11 marks the 1918 armistice ending fighting in WWI?

source.notes: 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month, 1918.

questions.q: Whose famous Gettysburg Address was delivered on November 19, 1863?

source.notes: 'Four score and seven years ago…' — 272 words long.

questions.q: What season is November in the Northern Hemisphere?

source.original_a: First hard frost of the season
source.notes: Spring in the Southern Hemisphere.

questions.q: Which pre-Christmas U.S. shopping holiday — the Friday after Thanksgiving — kicks off the season?

source.notes: Followed by Small Business Saturday and Cyber Monday.

questions.q: Which scientific writing month encourages writers to draft a 50,000-word novel during November?

source.notes: Founded 1999. Goal: 50,000 words in 30 days.

questions.q: Which two zodiac signs cover most of November?

source.notes: Scorpio runs through Nov 21; Sagittarius begins Nov 22.
